1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
5		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
6		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
7	)
8	
9	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
10	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
11	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
12	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
13	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
14	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
15		cmd := &cobra.Command{
16			Use:   "work",
17			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
18			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
19	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
20	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
21	
22	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
23	constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate these
24	values or branch on them; the agent owns routing within the requested power
25	bounds.
26	
27	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
28	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
29	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
30	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
31	
32	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
33	  drained     — no ready beads remain
34	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
35	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
36	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
37	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
38	
39	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
40	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
41	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
42	`,
43			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
44	  ddx work
45	
46	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
47	  ddx work --once
48	
49	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
50	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
51	
52	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
53	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
54	
55	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
56	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
57	
58	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
59	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
60			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
61			RunE: f.runWork,
62		}
63	
64		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
65		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
66		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
67		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
68		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
69		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
70		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
71		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
72		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
73		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
74		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
75		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 0, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits. Set 30s to keep the worker alive across empty polls.")
76		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
77		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
78		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
81		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
82		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
83		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
84		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
85		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
86		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
87			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
88		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
89		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
90		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
91		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
92	
93		// Register "ddx work plan" as a subcommand of "ddx work".
94		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
95	
96		return cmd
97	}
98	
99	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
100	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
101	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
102	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
103	// by this layer.
104	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
105		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
106	}

3	import (

9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
50	type BeadEvent struct {
51		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
52		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
53		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
55		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks a bead that is not eligible for agent dispatch —
65		// either decomposed into child beads by the triage gate or parked for
66		// human intervention after a triage-overflow event. Blocked beads are
67		// excluded from ReadyExecution and will not be auto-dispatched.
68		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
69		// StatusProposed marks an operator-prompt bead awaiting approval. It is
70		// excluded from execute-loop drain until transitioned to open via the
71		// approval flow, or to cancelled when the operator declines it.
72		StatusProposed = "proposed"
73		// StatusCancelled marks a bead that was rejected before any execution
74		// took place (e.g. an operator-prompt bead the user chose not to run).
75		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
76	)
77	
78	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
79	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
80	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
81	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
82	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
83	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
84	// will fail CI.
85	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{
86		StatusOpen,
87		StatusInProgress,
88		StatusClosed,
89		StatusBlocked,
90		StatusProposed,
91		StatusCancelled,
92	}
93	
94	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
95	// in CanonicalStatuses.
96	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
97		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
98			if c == s {
99				return true
100			}
101		}
102		return false
103	}
104	
105	// IssueType constants
106	const (
107		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
108		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
109		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
110	)
111	
112	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
113	const (
114		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
115		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
116		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
117		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
118		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
119		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
120		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
121	)
122	
123	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
124	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
125	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
126	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
127	//
128	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
129	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
130	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
131	//
132	// Allow/deny matrix:
133	//
134	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
135	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
136	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
137	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
138	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
139		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
140			return nil
141		}
142		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
143			return nil
144		}
145		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
146	}
147	
148	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
149	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
150	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
151	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
152	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
153	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
154	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
155	//
156	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
157	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
158	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
159		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
160		title := body
161		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
162			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
163		}
164		if title == "" {
165			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
166		}
167		tier := defaultTier
168		if tier < MinPriority {
169			tier = MinPriority
170		}
171		if tier > MaxPriority {
172			tier = MaxPriority
173		}
174		return &Bead{
175			Title:       title,
176			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
177			Status:      StatusProposed,
178			Priority:    tier,
179			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
180			Description: body,
181			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
182		}
183	}
184	
185	// Default values
186	const (
187		DefaultType     = "task"
188		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
189		DefaultPriority = 2
190		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
191		MinPriority     = 0
192		MaxPriority     = 4
193	)
194	
195	// StatusCounts holds aggregate counts for a bead store.
196	type StatusCounts struct {
197		Open        int `json:"open"`
198		Closed      int `json:"closed"`
199		Blocked     int `json:"blocked"`
200		Ready       int `json:"ready"`
201		NeedsHuman  int `json:"needs_human"`
202		WorkerReady int `json:"worker_ready"`
203		Total       int `json:"total"`
204	}
205	
206	// Blocker kinds surfaced through BlockedAll. These strings are part of the
207	// external DDx contract (HELIX reads them to decide how to handle a blocker).
208	const (
209		BlockerKindDependency         = "dependency"
210		BlockerKindBlockedStatus      = "blocked-status"
211		BlockerKindRetryCooldown      = "retry-cooldown"
212		BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation = "needs-investigation"
213		BlockerKindNotEligible        = "not-execution-eligible"
214		BlockerKindSuperseded         = "superseded"
215		BlockerKindEpicOnly           = "epic-only"
216	)
217	
218	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
219	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an execute-loop cooldown has parked the
220	// bead until NextEligibleAt.
221	type Blocker struct {
222		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
223		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`
224		UnclosedDepIDs []string `json:"unclosed_dep_ids,omitempty"`
225		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
226		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`
227		Reason         string   `json:"reason,omitempty"`
228	}
229	
230	// BlockedBead pairs a bead with its blocker classification.
231	type BlockedBead struct {
232		Bead
233		Blocker Blocker `json:"blocker"`
234	}
235	
236	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
237	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
238		var ids []string
239		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
240			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
241		}
242		return ids
243	}
244	
245	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
246	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
247		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
248			if d.DependsOnID == id {
249				return true
250			}
251		}
252		return false
253	}
254	
255	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
256	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
257		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
258			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
259				return // already exists
260			}
261		}
262		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
263			IssueID:     b.ID,
264			DependsOnID: depID,
265			Type:        depType,
266			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
267		})
268	}
269	
270	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
271	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
272		var filtered []Dependency
273		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
274			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
275				filtered = append(filtered, d)
276			}
277		}
278		b.Dependencies = filtered
279	}

2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and

1	package bead
2	
3	import "io"
4	
5	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
6	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
7	// implement it. Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops,
8	// events, archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and
9	// are composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
10	type RawBackend interface {
11		Init() error
12		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
13		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
14		WithLock(fn func() error) error
15	}
16	
17	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
18	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
19	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
20	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
21	// JSONL export/import.
22	//
23	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
24	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
25	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
26	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
27	//
28	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
29	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
30	type Backend interface {
31		// Foundational
32		Init() error
33		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
34	
35		// CRUD
36		Create(b *Bead) error
37		Get(id string) (*Bead, error)
38		Update(id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error
39		Close(id string) error
40	
41		// Claim
42		Claim(id, assignee string) error
43		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
44		Unclaim(id string) error
45	
46		// Query
47		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
48		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
49		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
50	
51		// Dep ops
52		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
53		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
54		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
55	
56		// Events
57		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
58		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
59	
60		// Archive split
61		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
62		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
63	
64		// JSONL interchange
65		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
66		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
67	}
68

70	const (
71		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
72		BackendBD    = "bd"
73		BackendBR    = "br"
74	)
75

357	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
358	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
359	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
360	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
361		if exitCode != 0 {
362			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
363		}
364	
365		switch outcome {
366		case "merged":
367			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
368			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
369			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
370			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
371			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
372			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
373				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
374			}
375			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
376				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
377			}
378			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
379		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
380			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
381		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
382			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
383		case "error":
384			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
385		case "preserved":
386			switch reason {
387			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
388				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
389			case "post-run checks failed":
390				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
391			case RatchetPreserveReason:
392				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
393			case OperatorCancelReason:
394				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
395			default:
396				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
397					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
398				}
399				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
400				// explicitly requested no merge.
401				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
402			}
403		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
404			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
405		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
406			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
407		default:
408			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
409		}
410	}

26		// no-op pass-through (no bead in the store carries a requires-capability
27		// field that the worker checks at pick time).
28		Capabilities string
29	}
30	
31	// FilterDecision records whether a bead is eligible for the next claim.
32	type FilterDecision string
33	
34	const (
35		FilterDecisionNext     FilterDecision = "next claim"
36		FilterDecisionEligible FilterDecision = "eligible"
37		FilterDecisionSkipped  FilterDecision = "skipped"
38	)
39	
40	// QueueEntry is one row in the PreviewQueue result. Position is 1-based;
41	// QueueRank is surfaced for ranked beads; FilterDecision and Why explain why
42	// the picker would or would not pick this bead as the next claim.
43	type QueueEntry struct {
44		Position       int            `json:"position"`
45		BeadID         string         `json:"id"`
46		Title          string         `json:"title"`
47		Priority       int            `json:"priority"`
48		QueueRank      *int           `json:"queue_rank,omitempty"`
49		UpdatedAt      time.Time      `json:"updated_at"`
50		Status         string         `json:"status"`
51		FilterDecision FilterDecision `json:"filter_decision"`
52		Why            string         `json:"why"`
53	}
54	
55	// PreviewQueue returns the bead execution queue in picker order, annotated with
56	// filter decisions and skip reasons for each entry. It applies the same filter
57	// logic as ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate (label_filter intersection) but
58	// intentionally omits the per-Run attempted map (non-deterministic across runs).
59	//
60	// The returned slice is ordered identically to what ReadyExecution returns —
61	// priority asc, then queue-rank asc, then created_at asc, then id within a
62	// priority bucket. The first entry whose FilterDecision is FilterDecisionNext
63	// is the bead nextCandidate would return on a fresh Run (before any claim).
64	//
65	// Limit controls the maximum number of entries returned. 0 means no limit
66	// (return all entries from ReadyExecution).
67	func PreviewQueue(store PreviewQueueStore, filters PickerFilters, limit int) ([]QueueEntry, error) {
68		ready, err := store.ReadyExecution()
69		if err != nil {
70			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preview queue: %w", err)
71		}
72	
73		foundFirst := false
74		var entries []QueueEntry
75		for _, b := range ready {
76			entry := QueueEntry{
77				Position:  len(entries) + 1,
78				BeadID:    b.ID,
79				Title:     b.Title,
80				Priority:  b.Priority,
81				QueueRank: queueRankPtr(b.Extra["queue-rank"]),
82				UpdatedAt: b.UpdatedAt,
83				Status:    b.Status,
84			}
85	
86			// Apply label filter — same logic as nextCandidate.
87			if filters.LabelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(b.Labels, filters.LabelFilter) {
88				entry.FilterDecision = FilterDecisionSkipped
89				entry.Why = fmt.Sprintf("skipped: label_filter mismatch (bead labels=%v; worker filter=[%s])", b.Labels, filters.LabelFilter)
90				entries = append(entries, entry)
91				if limit > 0 && len(entries) >= limit {
92					break
93				}
94				continue
95			}
96	
97			// Capabilities filter — no-op today; reserved for future matching.
98			// When implemented, add a skip reason here mirroring label_filter.
99	
100			// This bead is eligible. The first eligible bead is the "next claim".
101			if !foundFirst {
102				foundFirst = true
103				entry.FilterDecision = FilterDecisionNext
104				entry.Why = "next claim"
105			} else {
106				entry.FilterDecision = FilterDecisionEligible
107				entry.Why = fmt.Sprintf("eligible (rank %d)", entry.Position)
108			}
109			entries = append(entries, entry)
110			if limit > 0 && len(entries) >= limit {
111				break
112			}
113		}
114	
115		return entries, nil
116	}
117	
118	func queueRankPtr(raw any) *int {
119		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(raw)
120		if !ok {
121			return nil
122		}
123		return &rank
124	}
125	
126	func parseQueueRank(raw any) (int, bool) {
127		switch v := raw.(type) {
128		case *int:
129			if v == nil {
130				return 0, false
131			}
132			return *v, true
133		case int:
134			return v, true
135		case int8:
136			return int(v), true
137		case int16:
138			return int(v), true
139		case int32:
140			return int(v), true
141		case int64:
142			return int(v), true
143		case uint:
144			return int(v), true
145		case uint8:
146			return int(v), true
147		case uint16:
148			return int(v), true
149		case uint32:
150			return int(v), true
151		case uint64:
152			return int(v), true
153		case float32:
154			return int(v), true
155		case float64:
156			return int(v), true
157		default:
158			return 0, false
159		}
160	}

20	// TestMain clears all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
21	// that tests invoking git commands operate on their own temp-dir repositories
22	// rather than any repository inherited from a hook invocation (e.g. GIT_DIR
23	// set by lefthook during pre-commit). Prefix scrub is important — lefthook
24	// sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS etc. that
25	// also leak into test subprocesses and corrupt the shared repo config.
26	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
27		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
30					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
31				}
32			}
33		}
34		os.Exit(m.Run())
35	}
36	
37	var (
38		testLibraryPath string
39		testLibraryOnce sync.Once
40	)
41	
42	// GetTestLibraryPath returns the absolute path to a bare git repository
43	// that mimics a real upstream library (like GitHub). This is the ONLY way
44	// tests should get the test library path.
45	func GetTestLibraryPath() string {
46		testLibraryOnce.Do(func() {
47			// Find the test fixtures directory
48			_, filename, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
49			cmdDir := filepath.Dir(filename)
50			fixtureDir := filepath.Join(cmdDir, "..", "test", "fixtures", "ddx-library")
51			fixtureDir, _ = filepath.Abs(fixtureDir)
52	
53			// Determine base temp directory: TMP -> TMPDIR -> /tmp
54			tempBase := "/tmp"
55			if tmp := os.Getenv("TMP"); tmp != "" {
56				tempBase = tmp
57			} else if tmpdir := os.Getenv("TMPDIR"); tmpdir != "" {
58				tempBase = tmpdir
59			}
60	
61			workingRepo := filepath.Join(tempBase, ".test-library")
62			bareRepo := filepath.Join(tempBase, ".test-library.git")
63			testLibraryPath = bareRepo
64	
65			// Check if we need to recreate (fixtures changed or doesn't exist)
66			needsRecreate := false
67			if stat, err := os.Stat(bareRepo); err != nil || !stat.IsDir() {
68				needsRecreate = true
69			} else if os.Getenv("CI") == "" {
70				// In local dev, check if any fixture is newer than the repo
71				repoModTime := stat.ModTime()
72				filepath.Walk(fixtureDir, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
73					if err == nil && !info.IsDir() && info.ModTime().After(repoModTime) {
74						needsRecreate = true
75					}
76					return nil
77				})
78			}
79	
80			if needsRecreate {
81				// Clean up old repos
82				os.RemoveAll(workingRepo)
83				os.RemoveAll(bareRepo)
84	
85				// Create working repo and copy fixtures
86				if err := os.MkdirAll(workingRepo, 0755); err != nil {
87					panic(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to create working repo: %v", err))
88				}
89	
90				if err := syncDirectory(fixtureDir, workingRepo); err != nil {
91					panic(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to copy fixtures: %v", err))
92				}
93	
94				// Initialize git repo
95				cmds := []struct {
96					args []string
97					dir  string
98				}{
99					{[]string{"git", "init", "-b", "master"}, workingRepo},
100					{[]string{"git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"}, workingRepo},
101					{[]string{"git", "config", "user.name", "Test User"}, workingRepo},
102					{[]string{"git", "add", "."}, workingRepo},
103					{[]string{"git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "Test fixture"}, workingRepo},
104					{[]string{"git", "init", "--bare", bareRepo}, ""},
105					{[]string{"git", "remote", "add", "origin", bareRepo}, workingRepo},
106					{[]string{"git", "push", "origin", "master"}, workingRepo},
107				}
108	
109				for _, c := range cmds {
110					cmd := exec.Command(c.args[0], c.args[1:]...)
111					if c.dir != "" {
112						cmd.Dir = c.dir
113					}
114					if output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
115						panic(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to run %v: %v\nOutput: %s", c.args, err, output))
116					}
117				}
118			}
119		})
120		return testLibraryPath
121	}
122	
123	// syncDirectory copies files from src to dst, preserving directory structure
124	func syncDirectory(src, dst string) error {
125		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
126			if err != nil {

334	func (te *TestEnvironment) InitWithDDx(flags ...string) {
335		te.t.Helper()
336	
337		// Check if we should skip git operations
338		hasNoGitFlag := false
339		for _, flag := range flags {
340			if flag == "--no-git" {
341				hasNoGitFlag = true
342				break
343			}
344		}
345	
346		// If git is initialized and we're not using --no-git, create initial commit
347		if te.GitInitialized && !hasNoGitFlag {
348			cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
349			te.CreateFile("README.md", "# Test Project")
350			gitAdd := exec.Command("git", "add", ".")
351			gitAdd.Dir = te.Dir
352			gitAdd.Env = cleanEnv
353			require.NoError(te.t, gitAdd.Run())
354			gitCommit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "-m", "Initial commit")
355			gitCommit.Dir = te.Dir
356			gitCommit.Env = cleanEnv
357			require.NoError(te.t, gitCommit.Run())
358		}
359	
360		// Default flags if none provided
361		if len(flags) == 0 {
362			if te.GitInitialized {
363				// Use --repository and --branch flags to specify test library
364				flags = []string{"--repository", te.TestLibraryURL, "--branch", "master", "--silent", "--skip-claude-injection"}

367				flags = []string{"--no-git", "--silent", "--skip-claude-injection"}
368			}

371		args := append([]string{"init"}, flags...)
372		output, err := te.RunCommand(args...)

374	}
